narrative:
First Warn Forecast: Friday, May 8, 2026
First Warn Headlines
Widespread showers and scattered thunderstorms are expected this afternoon and evening across Missouri and Illinois.
A few storms could produce small hail and gusty winds, especially across Central Missouri.
Saturday turns warmer with highs nearing 80 degrees before another cold front arrives late Saturday night into Sunday.
Cooler air returns Sunday into early next week with highs likely back in the 60s.

Today is shaping up to be one of those classic spring days where you may want the sunglasses, umbrella, and maybe even the “why is Missouri like this?” starter pack all within a few hours.

A weak frontal boundary sliding through the region will help spark showers and scattered thunderstorms by late morning into the afternoon hours. Rain chances will steadily increase through the day, and while widespread severe weather is not expected, a few storms could briefly become stronger, mainly west and north of the St. Louis metro into Central Missouri. Small hail and gusty winds would be the primary threats through the late afternoon and early evening hours.

The atmosphere simply lacks the deeper moisture and stronger instability needed for widespread severe storms. Dew points remain fairly modest in the 50s, and while there will be decent wind shear aloft, instability profiles remain weak and narrow. In weather terms, it is basically the atmosphere saying, “I’ll try… but not too hard.”

Rain and thunderstorm activity will gradually diminish later this evening and overnight as the disturbance shifts eastward into the Ohio Valley. Partial clearing overnight will allow temperatures to fall back into the upper 40s and lower 50s by Saturday morning.

Saturday will likely be the warmest day we have experienced so far this month. Southwest winds return, helping temperatures climb close to or just above 80 degrees across much of the region. It will be a beautiful spring day overall, though changes arrive quickly again Saturday night.

Another cold front will move through Saturday night into Sunday morning with additional scattered showers possible. Behind the front, much cooler air settles back into the region Sunday and early next week. Depending on how quickly clouds and lingering rain exit Sunday, some areas may struggle to get much beyond the 60-degree mark.

Early next week looks quiet, dry, and seasonably cool before another system potentially arrives by the middle part of next week.
